caption: This lecture slide is from a course at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), specifically from the course "Advanced Data Structures" (6.851) offered in the Spring of 2012. The course is taught by Erik Demaine. The slide is titled "Session 7: Memory Hierarchy Models." The MIT logo is prominently displayed at the top left corner of the slide, and the MIT OpenCourseWare logo is at the bottom left corner. The Creative Commons license (CC BY-NC-SA) is also indicated at the bottom right corner of the slide.

The image is a lecture slide from a course at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT). The slide is from the course "6.851, Spring 2012: Advanced Data Structures," taught by Erik Demaine. The specific session being discussed is Session 7, which is titled "Memory Hierarchy Models." The slide includes the MIT logo at the top left corner and the MIT OpenCourseWare logo at the bottom left corner. The MIT logo consists of the letters "MIT" in white text on a black background. The MIT OpenCourseWare logo includes the text "MIT OpenCourseWare" and "Massachusetts

The lecture slide or whiteboard frame contains the following text:

```
TODAY: Memory Hierarchies I
- external-memory model
- cache-oblivious model
- cache-oblivious B-trees
```

The text is written in white chalk on a blackboard. The slide is titled "Memory Hierarchies I" and lists three topics that will be covered today:

1. **external-memory model**
2. **cache-oblivious model**
3. **cache-oblivious B-trees**
